commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR imports `isCliRuntimeAlias` and routes explicit CLI runtime aliases in `selectAgentHarnessDecision` to the built-in PI harness with a new selection reason.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main resolves a model-scoped `agentRuntime.id: "claude-cli"` into a non-auto runtime and `selectAgentHarnessDecision` throws `MissingAgentHarnessError` because no harness with that id is registered. I did not run a live compaction repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: There is a narrow, automatable repair: tighten the alias guard to provider-owned CLI aliases and add focused selection tests.

target.title: fix(harness): route CLI runtime aliases (claude-cli, google-gemini-cli) through PI harness
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When `agentRuntime.id` is set to a CLI runtime alias (`claude-cli`, `google-gemini-cli`), `selectAgentHarnessDecision` throws `MissingAgentHarnessError` because CLI aliases are not registered as plugin harnesses — they are dispatched internally via the cli-backend.
- **Why it matters:** Any session using `claude-cli` as `agentRuntime` crashes on compaction (context overflow) and on any other code path that calls `selectAgentHarness` directly. Sessions become permanently unrecoverable once context exceeds the limit.
- **What changed:** Added a guard in `selectAgentHarnessDecision` that detects CLI runtime aliases via `isCliRuntimeAlias()` and routes them to the PI harness (which dispatches through the cli-backend), parallel to the existing implicit-codex fallback.
- **What did NOT change:** Normal plugin harness selection, explicit `pi`/`auto` routing, codex implicit fallback, and the cli-backend dispatch path are all untouched.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Sessions with `agentRuntime: { id: "claude-cli" }` crash on compaction with `MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness "claude-cli" is not registered.`
- **Environment:** WSL2 Ubuntu, OpenClaw 2026.5.16-beta.7, gateway local, anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 + claude-opus-4-7 via claude-cli
- **Steps:** Rebuilt OpenClaw, restarted gateway, sent messages to a session at 143% context (1.5m/1.0m tokens) that previously failed compaction
- **Evidence after fix:**
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn: provider=claude-cli model=claude-opus-4-7 durationMs=135114 rawLines=322
[agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=sonnet ... activeSessions=2


No `MissingAgentHarnessError` in logs. Session compacted and continued successfully.
- **Before evidence:** `[diagnostic] lane task error: error="MissingAgentHarnessError: Requested agent harness \"claude-cli\" is not registered."` and `[ws] ⇄ res ✗ sessions.compact errorCode=UNAVAILABLE` on every compaction attempt.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `selectAgentHarnessDecision` has a special case for implicit `codex` but no equivalent for CLI runtime aliases. CLI aliases are not plugin harnesses — they are backend aliases dispatched by PI's cli-backend. The utility `isCliRuntimeAlias()` already existed in `model-runtime-aliases.ts` but wasn't called in the harness selection path.
- **Contributing context:** Any config using `agentRuntime: { id: "claude-cli" }` (the documented way to pin Claude models to Claude Code) hits this on context overflow.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[compaction] -> selectAgentHarness("claude-cli") -> MissingAgentHarnessError -> session dead

After:
[compaction] -> selectAgentHarness("claude-cli") -> isCliRuntimeAlias() -> PI harness -> cli-backend -> ok
Security Impact
New permissions/capabilities? No
Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
New/changed network calls? No
Command/tool execution surface changed? No
Data access scope changed? No
Compatibility / Migration
Backward compatible? Yes
Config/env changes? No
Migration needed? No
Risks and Mitigations
Risk: PI harness receiving a claude-cli runtime policy may behave differently in edge cases.
Mitigation: PI harness already dispatches claude-cli provider sessions through cli-backend (confirmed by logs); this patch aligns the compaction path with what the main session path already does.
target.author: skywolfdreamer
